nodes.label: ui5-dynamic-page-header
nodes.description:
Header of the DynamicPage.

### Overview

The DynamicPageHeader `ui5-dynamic-page-header` is part of the DynamicPage family
and is used to serve as header of the `DynamicPage`.

### Usage

The `DynamicPageHeader` can hold any layout control and has two states - expanded
and collapsed (snapped). The switching between these states happens when:
- the user scrolls below its bottom margin
- the user clicks on the `DynamicPageTitle`
- through the `DynamicPage` property `headerSnapped`

### Responsive Behavior

The responsive behavior of the `DynamicPageHeader` depends on the behavior of the
content that is displayed.

### Accessibility

The `DynamicPageHeader` provides an accessible label for the header region.

nodes.label: default
nodes.description: Defines the content of the Dynamic Page Header.

nodes.label: ui5-dynamic-page-header-actions
nodes.description:
### Overview

The `DynamicPageHeaderActions` component is part of the `DynamicPage`
family and is holding the action buttons behind the `DynamicPageTitle` and the `DynamicPageHeader`.

The "pin" action is used to attach the header to a certain state (expanded/collapsed).
The expand/collapse action is used to switch between the two states of `DynamicPageHeader`.

nodes.label: ui5-dynamic-page-title
nodes.description:
### Overview

Title of the `DynamicPage`.

The `DynamicPageTitle` component is part of the `DynamicPage`
family and is used to serve as title of the `DynamicPage`.

### Usage

The `DynamicPageTitle` can hold any component and displays the most important
information regarding the object that will always remain visible while scrolling.

**Note:** The `actions` slot accepts any UI5 web component, but it's
recommended to use `ui5-toolbar`.

The user can switch between the expanded/collapsed states of the
`DynamicPage` by clicking on the `DynamicPageTitle`
or by using the expand/collapse visual indicators, positioned at the bottom of the
`DynamicPageTitle` and the `DynamicPageHeader` inside `ui5-dynamic-page-header-actions`.

### Responsive Behavior

The responsive behavior of the `DynamicPageTitle` depends on the behavior of the
content that is displayed.

nodes.label: actionsBar
nodes.description: Defines the bar with actions in the Dynamic page title.

nodes.label: breadcrumbs
nodes.description: Defines the content of the breadcrumbs inside Dynamic Page Title.

nodes.label: default
nodes.description: Defines the content of the Dynamic page title.

nodes.label: heading
nodes.description:
Defines the content of the Heading of the Dynamic Page.

The font size of the title within the `heading` slot can be adjusted to the recommended values using the following CSS variables:

**Expanded:** `var(--sapObjectHeader_Title_FontSize)`

**Collapsed:** `var(--sapObjectHeader_Title_SnappedFontSize)`

nodes.label: navigationBar
nodes.description: Defines the bar with navigation actions in the Dynamic page title.

nodes.label: snappedHeading
nodes.description: Defines the heading that is shown only when the header is snapped.

nodes.label: snappedSubheading
nodes.description: Defines the content of the title that is shown only when the header is snapped.

nodes.label: snappedTitleOnMobile
nodes.description:
Defines the content of the snapped title on mobile devices.

This slot is displayed only when the `DynamicPageTitle` is in the snapped state on mobile devices.
It should be used to provide a simplified, single-line title that takes up less space on smaller screens.

**Note:**
- The content set in this slot **overrides** all other content set in the `DynamicPageTitle` slots when displayed.
- The slot is intended for a single `ui5-title` component.

nodes.label: subheading
nodes.description: Defines the content of the title that is shown only when the header is not snapped.

nodes.label: ui5-dynamic-side-content
nodes.description:
### Overview

The DynamicSideContent (`ui5-dynamic-side-content`) is a layout component that allows additional content
to be displayed in a way that flexibly adapts to different screen sizes. The side
content appears in a container next to or directly below the main content
(it doesn't overlay). When the side content is triggered, the main content becomes
narrower (if appearing side-by-side). The side content contains a separate scrollbar
when appearing next to the main content.

### Usage

*When to use?*

Use this component if you want to display relevant information that is not critical
for users to complete a task. Users should have access to all the key functions and
critical information in the app even if they do not see the side content. This is
important because on smaller screen sizes it may be difficult to display the side
content in a way that is easily accessible for the user.

*When not to use?*

Don't use it if you want to display navigation or critical information that prevents
users from completing a task when they have no access to the side content.

### Responsive Behavior

Screen width \> 1440px

- Main vs. side content ratio is 75 vs. 25 percent (with a minimum of 320px
each).
- If the application defines a trigger, the side content can be hidden.

Screen width \<\= 1440px and \> 1024px

- Main vs. side content ratio is 66.666 vs. 33.333 percent (with a minimum of
320px each). If the side content width falls below 320 px, it automatically slides
under the main content, unless the app development team specifies that it should
disappear.

Screen width \<\= 1024px and \> 720px

- The side content ratio is fixed to 340px, and the main content takes the rest
of the width. Only if the `sideContentFallDown` is set to `OnMinimumWidth`
and screen width is \<\= 960px and \> 720px the side content falls below the main content.

Screen width \<\= 720px (for example on a mobile device)

- In this case, the side content automatically disappears from the screen (unless
specified to stay under the content by setting of `sideContentVisibility`
property to `AlwaysShow`) and can be triggered from a pre-set trigger
(specified within the app). When the side content is triggered, it replaces the main
content. We recommend that you always place the trigger for the side content in the
same location, such as in the app footer.

A special case allows switching the comparison mode between the main and side content.
In this case, the screen is split into 50:50 percent for main vs. side content. The
responsive behavior of the equal split is the same as in the standard view - the
side content disappears on screen widths of less than 720 px and can only be
viewed by triggering it.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/DynamicSideContent.js";`

nodes.label: default
nodes.description: Defines the main content.

nodes.label: sideContent
nodes.description: Defines the side content.

nodes.label: accessibilityAttributes
nodes.description:
Defines additional accessibility attributes on different areas of the component.

The accessibilityAttributes object has the following fields:

 - **mainContent**: `mainContent.ariaLabel` defines the aria-label of the main content area. Accepts any string.
 - **sideContent**: `sideContent.ariaLabel` defines the aria-label of the side content area. Accepts any string.

nodes.label: equalSplit
nodes.description:
Defines whether the component is in equal split mode. In this mode, the side and
the main content take 50:50 percent of the container on all screen sizes
except for phone, where the main and side contents are switching visibility
using the toggle method.

nodes.label: hideMainContent
nodes.description: Defines the visibility of the main content.

nodes.label: hideSideContent
nodes.description: Defines the visibility of the side content.

nodes.label: sideContentFallDown
nodes.description: Defines on which breakpoints the side content falls down below the main content.

nodes.label: sideContentPosition
nodes.description:
Defines whether the side content is positioned before the main content (left side
in LTR mode), or after the the main content (right side in LTR mode).

nodes.label: sideContentVisibility
nodes.description: Defines on which breakpoints the side content is visible.

nodes.label: layout-change
nodes.description: Fires when the current breakpoint has been changed.

nodes.label: ui5-filter-item
nodes.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-filter-item` component defines the filtering criteria for data in `ui5-view-settings-dialog`.
It represents a single filter category that contains multiple filter options that users can select.

### Usage

The `ui5-filter-item` is used within the `ui5-view-settings-dialog` to provide filtering options.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/FilterItem.js";`

nodes.label: values
nodes.description: Defines the filter options available for this filter category.

nodes.label: additionalText
nodes.description:
Defines the additional text of the filter item.
This text is typically used to show the number of selected filter options within this category.

nodes.label: text
nodes.description: Defines the text of the filter item.

nodes.label: ui5-filter-item-option
nodes.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-filter-item-option` component defines individual filter values within a `ui5-filter-item`.
It represents a single selectable option that users can choose to filter data.

### Usage

The `ui5-filter-item-option` is used as a child component within `ui5-filter-item` in the context
of `ui5-view-settings-dialog`. Each option represents a specific value that can be used for filtering

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/FilterItemOption.js";`

nodes.label: selected
nodes.description: Defines if the filter option is selected.

nodes.label: text
nodes.description: Defines the text of the filter option.

nodes.label: ui5-flexible-column-layout
nodes.description:
### Overview

The `FlexibleColumnLayout` implements the list-detail-detail paradigm by displaying up to three pages in separate columns.
There are several possible layouts that can be changed either with the component API, or by dragging the column separators.

### Usage

Use this component for applications that need to display several logical levels of related information side by side (e.g. list of items, item, sub-item, etc.).
The Component is flexible in a sense that the application can focus the user's attention on one particular column.

### Responsive Behavior

The `FlexibleColumnLayout` automatically displays the maximum possible number of columns based on `layout` property and the window size.
The component would display 1 column for window size smaller than 599px, up to two columns between 599px and 1023px,
and 3 columns for sizes bigger than 1023px.

**Note:** When the component displays more than one column, the minimal width of each column is 248px. Consequently, when the user drags a column separator to resize the columns, the minimal allowed width of any resized column is 248px.

### Keyboard Handling

#### Basic Navigation

When a column separator is focused,  the following keyboard
shortcuts allow the user to resize the columns and change the layout:

- [Shift] + [Left] or [Shift] + [Right] - Moves the separator to the left or right, which resizes the columns accordingly.
- [Left] or [Right] - Moves the separator to the left or right with a bigger step, which resizes the columns accordingly.
- [Home] - Moves the separator to the start position.
- [End] - Moves the separator to the end position.
- This component provides a build in fast navigation group which can be used via [F6] / [Shift] + [F6] / [Ctrl] + [Alt/Option] / [Down] or [Ctrl] + [Alt/Option] + [Up].
In order to use this functionality, you need to import the following module:
`import "@ui5/webcomponents-base/dist/features/F6Navigation.js"`

#### Fast Navigation
This component provides a build in fast navigation group which can be used via [F6] / [Shift] + [F6] / [Ctrl] + [Alt/Option] / [Down] or [Ctrl] + [Alt/Option] + [Up].
In order to use this functionality, you need to import the following module:
`import "@ui5/webcomponents-base/dist/features/F6Navigation.js"`

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/FlexibleColumnLayout.js";`

nodes.label: endColumn
nodes.description: Defines the content in the end column.

nodes.label: midColumn
nodes.description: Defines the content in the middle column.

nodes.label: startColumn
nodes.description: Defines the content in the start column.

nodes.label: accessibilityAttributes
nodes.description:
Defines additional accessibility attributes on different areas of the component.

The accessibilityAttributes object has the following fields,
where each field is an object supporting one or more accessibility attributes:

 - **startColumn**: `startColumn.role` and `startColumn.name`.
 - **midColumn**: `midColumn.role` and `midColumn.name`.
 - **endColumn**: `endColumn.role` and `endColumn.name`.
 - **startSeparator**: `startSeparator.role` and `startSeparator.name`.
 - **endSeparator**: `endSeparator.role` and `endSeparator.name`.

The accessibility attributes support the following values:

- **role**: Defines the accessible ARIA landmark role of the area.
Accepts the following values: `none`, `complementary`, `contentinfo`, `main` or `region`.

- **name**: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the area.
Accepts any string.

nodes.label: columnLayout
nodes.description:
Returns the current column layout, based on both the `layout` property and the screen size.

**For example:** ["67%", "33%", 0], ["100%", 0, 0], ["25%", "50%", "25%"], etc,
where the numbers represents the width of the start, middle and end columns.

nodes.label: disableResizing
nodes.description: Specifies if the user is allowed to change the columns layout by dragging the separator between the columns.

nodes.label: endColumnVisible
nodes.description: Returns if the `end` column is visible.

nodes.label: layout
nodes.description:
Defines the columns layout and their proportion.

**Note:** The layout also depends on the screen size - one column for screens smaller than 599px,
two columns between 599px and 1023px and three columns for sizes bigger than 1023px.

**For example:** layout=`TwoColumnsStartExpanded` means the layout will display up to two columns
in 67%/33% proportion.

nodes.label: layoutsConfiguration
nodes.description:
Allows to customize the column proportions per screen size and layout.
If no custom proportion provided for a specific layout, the default will be used.

**Notes:**

- The proportions should be given in percentages. For example ["30%", "40%", "30%"], ["70%", "30%", 0], etc.
- The proportions should add up to 100%.
- Hidden columns are marked as "0px", e.g. ["0px", "70%", "30%"]. Specifying 0 or "0%" for hidden columns is also valid.
- If the proportions do not match the layout (e.g. if provided proportions ["70%", "30%", "0px"] for "OneColumn" layout), then the default proportions will be used instead.
- Whenever the user drags the columns separator to resize the columns, the `layoutsConfiguration` object will be updated with the user-specified proportions for the given layout (and the `layout-configuration-change` event will be fired).
- No custom configuration available for the phone screen size, as the default of 100% column width is always used there.

nodes.label: midColumnVisible
nodes.description: Returns if the `middle` column is visible.

nodes.label: startColumnVisible
nodes.description: Returns if the `start` column is visible.

nodes.label: visibleColumns
nodes.description: Returns the number of currently visible columns.

nodes.label: layout-change
nodes.description:
Fired when the layout changes via user interaction by dragging the separators
or by changing the component size due to resizing.

nodes.label: layout-configuration-change
nodes.description:
Fired when the `layoutsConfiguration` changes via user interaction by dragging the separators.

**Note:** The `layout-configuration-change` event is in an experimental state and is a subject to change.

nodes.label: ui5-group-item
nodes.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-group-item` component defines the grouping criteria for data in `ui5-view-settings-dialog`.
It represents a single group option that users can select to organize data into logical groups.

### Usage

The `ui5-group-item` is used within the `ui5-view-settings-dialog` to provide grouping options.
Each group item represents a column or field by which data can be grouped.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/GroupItem.js";`

nodes.label: selected
nodes.description: Defines if the group item is selected.

nodes.label: text
nodes.description: Defines the text of the group item.

nodes.label: ui5-illustrated-message
nodes.description:
### Overview
An IllustratedMessage is a recommended combination of a solution-oriented message, an engaging
illustration, and conversational tone to better communicate an empty or a success state than just show
a message alone.

Each illustration has default internationalised title and subtitle texts. Also they can be managed with
`titleText` and `subtitleText` properties.

To display the desired illustration, use the `name` property, where you can find the list of all available illustrations.

**Note:** By default the “BeforeSearch” illustration is loaded. To use other illustrations, make sure you import them in addition, for example:

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/illustrations/NoData.js"`

**Note:** Illustrations starting with the “Tnt” prefix are part of another illustration set. For example to use the “TntSuccess” illustration, add the following import::

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/illustrations/tnt/Success.js"`

### Structure
The IllustratedMessage consists of the following elements, which are displayed below each other in the following order:

- Illustration
- Title
- Subtitle
- Actions

### Usage
`ui5-illustrated-message` is meant to be used inside container component, for example a `ui5-card`,
a `ui5-dialog` or a `ui5-page`

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/IllustratedMessage.js";`

nodes.label: default
nodes.description:
Defines the component actions.

**Note:** Not displayed when the `design` property is set to `Base`.

nodes.label: subtitle
nodes.description:
Defines the subtitle of the component.

**Note:** Using this slot, the default subtitle text of illustration and the value of `subtitleText` property will be overwritten.

nodes.label: title
nodes.description:
Defines the title of the component.

**Note:** Using this slot, the default title text of illustration and the value of `title` property will be overwritten.

nodes.label: accessibleNameRef
nodes.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that label the component.

nodes.label: decorative
nodes.description:
Defines whether the illustration is decorative.

When set to `true`, the attributes `role="presentation"` and `aria-hidden="true"` are applied to the SVG element.

nodes.label: design
nodes.description:
Determines which illustration breakpoint variant is used.

As `IllustratedMessage` adapts itself around the `Illustration`, the other
elements of the component are displayed differently on the different breakpoints/illustration designs.

nodes.label: name
nodes.description:
Defines the illustration name that will be displayed in the component.

Example:

`name='BeforeSearch'`, `name='UnableToUpload'`, etc..

**Note:** To use the TNT illustrations,
you need to set the `tnt` or `Tnt` prefix in front of the icon's name.

Example:

`name='tnt/Avatar'` or `name='TntAvatar'`.

**Note:** By default the `BeforeSearch` illustration is loaded.
When using an illustration type, other than the default, it should be loaded in addition:

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/illustrations/NoData.js";`

For TNT illustrations:

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/illustrations/tnt/SessionExpired.js";`

nodes.label: subtitleText
nodes.description:
Defines the subtitle of the component.

**Note:** Using this property, the default subtitle text of illustration will be overwritten.

**Note:** Using `subtitle` slot, the default of this property will be overwritten.

nodes.label: titleText
nodes.description:
Defines the title of the component.

**Note:** Using this property, the default title text of illustration will be overwritten.

nodes.label: subtitle
nodes.description: Used to style the subtitle wrapper of the `ui5-illustrated-message`

nodes.label: ui5-media-gallery
nodes.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-media-gallery` component allows the user to browse through multimedia items. Currently,
the supported items are images and videos. The items should be defined using the `ui5-media-gallery-item`
component.

The items are initially displayed as thumbnails. When the user selects a thumbnail, the corresponding item
is displayed in larger size.

The component is responsive by default and adjusts the position of the menu with respect to viewport size,
but the application is able to further customize the layout via the provided API.

### Keyboard Handling
The `ui5-media-gallery` provides advanced keyboard handling.

When the thumbnails menu is focused the following keyboard
shortcuts allow the user to navigate through the thumbnail items:

- [Up] or [Down] - Navigates up and down the items
- [Home] - Navigates to first item
- [End] - Navigates to the last item
- [Space], [Enter] - Selects an item

### ES6 Module Import
`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/MediaGallery.js";`

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/MediaGalleryItem.js";`

nodes.label: default
nodes.description:
Defines the component items.

**Note:** Only one selected item is allowed.

**Note:** Use the `ui5-media-gallery-item` component to define the desired items.

nodes.label: interactiveDisplayArea
nodes.description:
If enabled, a `display-area-click` event is fired
when the user clicks or taps on the display area.

The display area is the central area that contains
the enlarged content of the currently selected item.

nodes.label: layout
nodes.description: Determines the layout of the component.

nodes.label: menuHorizontalAlign
nodes.description:
Determines the horizontal alignment of the thumbnails menu
vs. the central display area.

nodes.label: menuVerticalAlign
nodes.description:
Determines the vertical alignment of the thumbnails menu
vs. the central display area.

nodes.label: showAllThumbnails
nodes.description:
If set to `true`, all thumbnails are rendered in a scrollable container.
If `false`, only up to five thumbnails are rendered, followed by
an overflow button that shows the count of the remaining thumbnails.

nodes.label: display-area-click
nodes.description:
Fired when the display area is clicked.
The display area is the central area that contains
the enlarged content of the currently selected item.

nodes.label: overflow-click
nodes.description: Fired when the thumbnails overflow button is clicked.

nodes.label: selection-change
nodes.description: Fired when selection is changed by user interaction.

nodes.label: ui5-media-gallery-item
nodes.description:
### Overview
The `ui5-media-gallery-item` web component represents the items displayed in the
`ui5-media-gallery` web component.

**Note:** `ui5-media-gallery-item` is not supported when used outside of `ui5-media-gallery`.

### Keyboard Handling
The `ui5-media-gallery` provides advanced keyboard handling.
When focused, the user can use the following keyboard
shortcuts in order to perform a navigation:

- [Space] / [Enter] or [Return] - Trigger `ui5-click` event

### ES6 Module Import
`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/MediaGalleryItem.js";` (comes with `ui5-media-gallery`)

nodes.label: default
nodes.description: Defines the content of the component.

nodes.label: thumbnail
nodes.description: Defines the content of the thumbnail.

nodes.label: disabled
nodes.description: Defines whether the component is in disabled state.

nodes.label: layout
nodes.description: Determines the layout of the item container.

nodes.label: selected
nodes.description: Defines the selected state of the component.

nodes.label: ui5-navigation-layout
nodes.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-navigation-layout` is a container component that can be used to
create a layout with a header, a side navigation and a content area.

### Usage

Use the `ui5-navigation-layout` to create whole screen of an application with vertical navigation.

### Responsive Behavior

On larger screens with a width of 600px or more, excluding mobile phone devices, the side navigation is visible
by default and can be expanded or collapsed using the `mode` property.
On mobile phone devices and screens with a width of 599px or less, the side navigation is hidden by
default and can be displayed using the `mode` property.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/NavigationLayout.js";`

nodes.label: default
nodes.description: Defines the content.

nodes.label: header
nodes.description: Defines the header.

nodes.label: sideContent
nodes.description: Defines the side content.

nodes.label: mode
nodes.description: Specifies the navigation layout mode.

nodes.label: ui5-navigation-menu
nodes.description:
### Overview

`ui5-navigation-menu` component represents a hierarchical menu structure, inherits all the functionality of `ui5-menu`.

### Usage

`ui5-navigation-menu` contains `ui5-navigation-menu-item` components.
An arbitrary hierarchy structure can be represented by recursively nesting navigation menu items.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/NavigationMenu.js";`

nodes.label: default
nodes.description:
Defines the items of this component.

**Note:** Use `ui5-navigation-menu-item` for the intended design.

nodes.label: headerText
nodes.description: Defines the header text of the menu (displayed on mobile).

nodes.label: horizontalAlign
nodes.description: Determines the horizontal alignment of the menu relative to its opener control.

nodes.label: loading
nodes.description: Defines if a loading indicator would be displayed inside the corresponding ui5-menu popover.

nodes.label: loadingDelay
nodes.description: Defines the delay in milliseconds, after which the loading indicator will be displayed inside the corresponding ui5-menu popover.

nodes.label: open
nodes.description: Indicates if the menu is open.

nodes.label: opener
nodes.description:
Defines the ID or DOM Reference of the element at which the menu is shown.
When using this attribute in a declarative way, you must only use the `id` (as a string) of the element at which you want to show the popover.
You can only set the `opener` attribute to a DOM Reference when using JavaScript.

nodes.label: placement
nodes.description: Determines on which side the component is placed at.

nodes.label: before-close
nodes.description: Fired before the menu is closed. This event can be cancelled, which will prevent the menu from closing.

nodes.label: before-open
nodes.description:
Fired before the menu is opened. This event can be cancelled, which will prevent the menu from opening.

**Note:** Since 1.14.0 the event is also fired before a sub-menu opens.

nodes.label: close
nodes.description: Fired after the menu is closed.

nodes.label: item-click
nodes.description:
Fired when an item is being clicked.

**Note:** Since 1.17.0 the event is preventable, allowing the menu to remain open after an item is pressed.

nodes.label: open
nodes.description: Fired after the menu is opened.

nodes.label: ui5-navigation-menu-item
nodes.description:
### Overview

`ui5-navigation-menu-item` is the item to use inside a `ui5-navigation-menu`.
An arbitrary hierarchy structure can be represented by recursively nesting navigation menu items.

### Usage

`ui5-navigation-menu-item` represents a node in a `ui5-navigation-menu`. The navigation menu itself is rendered as a list,
and each `ui5-navigation-menu-item` is represented by a list item in that list. Therefore, you should only use
`ui5-navigation-menu-item` directly in your apps. The `ui5-li` list item is internal for the list, and not intended for public use.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/NavigationMenuItem.js";`

nodes.label: default
nodes.description:
Defines the items of this component.

**Note:** The slot can hold menu item and menu separator items.

If there are items added to this slot, an arrow will be displayed at the end
of the item in order to indicate that there are items added. In that case components added
to `endContent` slot or `additionalText` content will not be displayed.

The priority of what will be displayed at the end of the menu item is as follows:
sub-menu arrow (if there are items added in `items` slot) -> components added in `endContent` -> text set to `additionalText`.

nodes.label: deleteButton
nodes.description:
Defines the delete button, displayed in "Delete" mode.
**Note:** While the slot allows custom buttons, to match
design guidelines, please use the `ui5-button` component.
**Note:** When the slot is not present, a built-in delete button will be displayed.

nodes.label: endContent
nodes.description:
Defines the components that should be displayed at the end of the menu item.

**Note:** It is highly recommended to slot only components of type `ui5-button`,`ui5-link`
or `ui5-icon` in order to preserve the intended design. If there are components added to this slot,
and there is text set in `additionalText`, it will not be displayed. If there are items added to `items` slot,
nether `additionalText` nor components added to this slot would be displayed.

The priority of what will be displayed at the end of the menu item is as follows:
sub-menu arrow (if there are items added in `items` slot) -> components added in `endContent` -> text set to `additionalText`.

Application developers are responsible for ensuring that interactive elements placed in the `endContent` slot
have the correct accessibility behaviour, including their enabled or disabled states.
The menu does not manage these aspects when the menu item state changes.

nodes.label: href
nodes.description:
Defines the link target URI. Supports standard hyperlink behavior.
If a JavaScript action should be triggered,
this should not be set, but instead an event handler
for the `click` event should be registered.

nodes.label: target
nodes.description:
Defines the component target.

**Notes:**

- `_self`
- `_top`
- `_blank`
- `_parent`
- `_search`

**This property must only be used when the `href` property is set.**

nodes.label: accessibilityAttributes
nodes.description:
Defines the additional accessibility attributes that will be applied to the component.
The following fields are supported:

- **ariaKeyShortcuts**: Indicated the availability of a keyboard shortcuts defined for the menu item.

- **role**: Defines the role of the menu item. If not set, menu item will have default role="menuitem".

nodes.label: accessibleName
nodes.description: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the component.

nodes.label: additionalText
nodes.description:
Defines the `additionalText`, displayed in the end of the menu item.

**Note:** The additional text will not be displayed if there are items added in `items` slot or there are
components added to `endContent` slot.

The priority of what will be displayed at the end of the menu item is as follows:
sub-menu arrow (if there are items added in `items` slot) -> components added in `endContent` -> text set to `additionalText`.

nodes.label: checked
nodes.description:
Defines whether menu item is in checked state.

**Note:** checked state is only taken into account when menu item is added to menu item group
with `checkMode` other than `None`.

**Note:** A checked menu item has a checkmark displayed at its end.

nodes.label: disabled
nodes.description:
Defines whether menu item is in disabled state.

**Note:** A disabled menu item is noninteractive.

nodes.label: highlight
nodes.description:
Defines the highlight state of the list items.
Available options are: `"None"` (by default), `"Positive"`, `"Critical"`, `"Information"` and `"Negative"`.

nodes.label: icon
nodes.description:
Defines the icon to be displayed as graphical element within the component.
The SAP-icons font provides numerous options.

**Example:**

See all the available icons in the [Icon Explorer](https://sdk.openui5.org/test-resources/sap/m/demokit/iconExplorer/webapp/index.html).

nodes.label: loading
nodes.description:
Defines the delay in milliseconds, after which the loading indicator will be displayed inside the corresponding menu popover.

**Note:** If set to `true` a busy indicator component will be displayed into the related one to the current menu item sub-menu popover.

nodes.label: loadingDelay
nodes.description: Defines the delay in milliseconds, after which the loading indicator will be displayed inside the corresponding menu popover.

nodes.label: navigated
nodes.description:
The navigated state of the list item.
If set to `true`, a navigation indicator is displayed at the end of the list item.

nodes.label: selected
nodes.description: Defines the selected state of the component.

nodes.label: text
nodes.description: Defines the text of the tree item.

nodes.label: tooltip
nodes.description: Defines the text of the tooltip for the menu item.

nodes.label: type
nodes.description:
Defines the visual indication and behavior of the list items.
Available options are `Active` (by default), `Inactive`, `Detail` and `Navigation`.

**Note:** When set to `Active` or `Navigation`, the item will provide visual response upon press and hover,
while with type `Inactive` and `Detail` - will not.

nodes.label: before-close
nodes.description: Fired before the menu is closed. This event can be cancelled, which will prevent the menu from closing.

nodes.label: before-open
nodes.description:
Fired before the menu is opened. This event can be cancelled, which will prevent the menu from opening.

**Note:** Since 1.14.0 the event is also fired before a sub-menu opens.

nodes.label: check
nodes.description: Fired when an item is checked or unchecked.

nodes.label: close
nodes.description: Fired after the menu is closed.

nodes.label: detail-click
nodes.description: Fired when the user clicks on the detail button when type is `Detail`.

nodes.label: open
nodes.description: Fired after the menu is opened.

nodes.label: ui5-notification-list
nodes.description:
### Overview
The `ui5-notification-list` web component represents
a container for `ui5-li-notification-group` and `ui5-li-notification`.

### Keyboard Handling

#### Basic Navigation
The `ui5-notification-list` provides advanced keyboard handling.
When a list is focused the user can use the following keyboard
shortcuts in order to perform a navigation:

- [Up] or [Left] - Navigates up the items
- [Down] or [Right] - Navigates down the items
- [Home] - Navigates to first item
- [End] - Navigates to the last item

#### Fast Navigation
This component provides a build in fast navigation group which can be used via [F6] / [Shift] + [F6] / [Ctrl] + [Alt/Option] / [Down] or [Ctrl] + [Alt/Option] + [Up].
In order to use this functionality, you need to import the following module:
`import "@ui5/webcomponents-base/dist/features/F6Navigation.js"`

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/NotificationList.js";``

nodes.label: default
nodes.description: Defines the items of the component.

nodes.label: noDataText
nodes.description: Defines the text that is displayed when the component contains no items.

nodes.label: item-click
nodes.description: Fired when an item is clicked.

nodes.label: item-close
nodes.description: Fired when the `Close` button of any item is clicked.

nodes.label: item-toggle
nodes.description: Fired when an item is toggled.

nodes.label: ui5-li-notification-group
nodes.description:
### Overview
The `ui5-li-notification-group` is a special type of list item,
that unlike others can group items within self, usually `ui5-li-notification` items.

The component consists of:

- `Toggle` button to expand and collapse the group
- `TitleText` to entitle the group
- Items of the group

### Usage
The component should be used inside a `ui5-notification-list`.

### Keyboard Handling
The `ui5-li-notification-group` provides advanced keyboard handling.
This component provides fast navigation when the header is focused using the following keyboard shortcuts:

- [Space] - toggles expand / collapse of the group
- [Plus] - expands the group
- [Minus] - collapses the group
- [Right] - expands the group
- [Left] - collapses the group

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/NotificationListGroupItem.js";`

nodes.label: default
nodes.description:
Defines the items of the `ui5-li-notification-group`,
usually `ui5-li-notification` items.

nodes.label: collapsed
nodes.description: Defines if the group is collapsed or expanded.

nodes.label: growing
nodes.description:
Defines whether the component will have growing capability by pressing a `More` button.
When button is pressed `load-more` event will be fired.

nodes.label: loading
nodes.description: Defines if a busy indicator would be displayed over the item.

nodes.label: loadingDelay
nodes.description: Defines the delay in milliseconds, after which the busy indicator will show up for this component.

nodes.label: read
nodes.description:
Defines if the `notification` is new or has been already read.

**Note:** if set to `false` the `titleText` has bold font,
if set to true - it has a normal font.

nodes.label: titleText
nodes.description: Defines the `titleText` of the item.

nodes.label: load-more
nodes.description: Fired when additional items are requested.

nodes.label: toggle
nodes.description: Fired when the `ui5-li-notification-group` is expanded/collapsed by user interaction.

nodes.label: ui5-notification-group-list
nodes.description:
Internal `ui5-li-notification-group-list` component,
that is used to support keyboard navigation of the notification group internal list.

nodes.label: default
nodes.description:
Defines the items of the component.

**Note:** Use `ui5-li`, `ui5-li-custom`, and `ui5-li-group` for the intended design.

nodes.label: header
nodes.description:
Defines the component header.

**Note:** When `header` is set, the
`headerText` property is ignored.

nodes.label: accessibilityAttributes
nodes.description:
Defines additional accessibility attributes on different areas of the component.

The accessibilityAttributes object has the following field:

 - **growingButton**: `growingButton.name`, `growingButton.description`.

 The accessibility attributes support the following values:

- **name**: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the growing button.
Accepts any string.

- **description**: Defines the accessible ARIA description of the growing button.
Accepts any string.

 **Note:** The `accessibilityAttributes` property is in an experimental state and is a subject to change.

nodes.label: accessibleDescription
nodes.description: Defines the accessible description of the component.

nodes.label: accessibleDescriptionRef
nodes.description: Defines the IDs of the elements that describe the component.

nodes.label: accessibleName
nodes.description: Defines the accessible name of the component.

nodes.label: accessibleNameRef
nodes.description: Defines the IDs of the elements that label the component.

nodes.label: accessibleRole
nodes.description: Defines the accessible role of the component.

nodes.label: footerText
nodes.description: Defines the footer text.

nodes.label: growing
nodes.description:
Defines whether the component will have growing capability either by pressing a `More` button,
or via user scroll. In both cases `load-more` event is fired.

**Restrictions:** `growing="Scroll"` is not supported for Internet Explorer,
on IE the component will fallback to `growing="Button"`.

nodes.label: growingButtonText
nodes.description:
Defines the text that will be displayed inside the growing button.

**Note:** If not specified a built-in text will be displayed.

**Note:** This property takes effect if the `growing` property is set to the `Button`.

nodes.label: headerText
nodes.description:
Defines the component header text.

**Note:** If `header` is set this property is ignored.

nodes.label: indent
nodes.description: Determines whether the component is indented.

nodes.label: listItems
nodes.description: Returns an array containing the list item instances without the groups in a flat structure.

nodes.label: loading
nodes.description: Defines if the component would display a loading indicator over the list.

nodes.label: loadingDelay
nodes.description: Defines the delay in milliseconds, after which the loading indicator will show up for this component.

nodes.label: noDataText
nodes.description: Defines the text that is displayed when the component contains no items.

nodes.label: selectionMode
nodes.description: Defines the selection mode of the component.

nodes.label: separators
nodes.description: Defines the item separator style that is used.

nodes.label: stickyHeader
nodes.description:
Indicates whether the List header is sticky or not.
If stickyHeader is set to true, then whenever you scroll the content or
the application, the header of the list will be always visible.

nodes.label: item-click
nodes.description:
Fired when an item is activated, unless the item's `type` property
is set to `Inactive`.

**Note**: This event is not triggered by interactions with selection components such as the checkboxes and radio buttons,
associated with non-default `selectionMode` values, or if any other **interactive** component
(such as a button or input) within the list item is directly clicked.

nodes.label: item-close
nodes.description:
Fired when the `Close` button of any item is clicked

**Note:** This event is only applicable to list items that can be closed (such as notification list items),
not to be confused with `item-delete`.

nodes.label: item-delete
nodes.description:
Fired when the Delete button of any item is pressed.

**Note:** A Delete button is displayed on each item,
when the component `selectionMode` property is set to `Delete`.

nodes.label: item-toggle
nodes.description:
Fired when the `Toggle` button of any item is clicked.

**Note:** This event is only applicable to list items that can be toggled (such as notification group list items).

nodes.label: load-more
nodes.description:
Fired when the user scrolls to the bottom of the list.

**Note:** The event is fired when the `growing='Scroll'` property is enabled.

nodes.label: move
nodes.description:
Fired when a movable list item is dropped onto a drop target.

**Note:** `move` event is fired only if there was a preceding `move-over` with prevented default action.

nodes.label: move-over
nodes.description:
Fired when a movable list item is moved over a potential drop target during a dragging operation.

If the new position is valid, prevent the default action of the event using `preventDefault()`.

nodes.label: selection-change
nodes.description:
Fired when selection is changed by user interaction
in `Single`, `SingleStart`, `SingleEnd` and `Multiple` selection modes.

nodes.label: growing-button
nodes.description: Used to style the button, that is used for growing of the component

nodes.label: growing-button-inner
nodes.description: Used to style the button inner element

nodes.label: ui5-notification-list-internal

Internal `ui5-li-notification-list-list` component,
that is used to support keyboard navigation of the notification list internal list.

nodes.label: ui5-li-notification
nodes.description:
### Overview
The `ui5-li-notification` is a type of list item, meant to display notifications.

The component has a rich set of various properties that allows the user to set `avatar`, `menu`, `titleText`, descriptive `content`
and `footnotes` to fully describe a notification.

The user can:

- display a `Close` button
- can control whether the `titleText` and `description` should wrap or truncate
and display a `ShowMore` button to switch between less and more information
- add actions by using the `ui5-menu` component

**Note:** Adding custom actions by using the `ui5-notification-action` component is deprecated as of version 2.0!

### Usage
The component should be used inside a `ui5-notification-list`.

### Keyboard Handling

#### Basic Navigation
The user can use the following keyboard shortcuts to perform actions (such as select, delete):

- [Enter] - select an item (trigger "item-click" event)
- [Delete] - close an item (trigger "item-close" event)

#### Fast Navigation
This component provides a fast navigation using the following keyboard shortcuts:

- [Shift] + [Enter] - 'More'/'Less' link will be triggered
- [Shift] + [F10] - 'Menu' (Actions) button will be triggered (clicked)

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/NotificationListItem.js";`

nodes.label: avatar
nodes.description:
Defines the avatar, displayed in the `ui5-li-notification`.

**Note:** Consider using the `ui5-avatar` to display icons, initials or images.

**Note:** In order to be complaint with the UX guidlines and for best experience,
we recommend using avatars with 2rem X 2rem in size (32px X 32px). In case you are using the `ui5-avatar`
you can set its `size` property to `XS` to get the required size - `<ui5-avatar size="XS"></ui5-avatar>`.

nodes.label: default
nodes.description:
Defines the content of the `ui5-li-notification`,
usually a description of the notification.

**Note:** Although this slot accepts HTML Elements, it is strongly recommended that you only use text in order to preserve the intended design.

nodes.label: footnotes
nodes.description: Defines the elements, displayed in the footer of the of the component.

nodes.label: menu
nodes.description:
Defines the menu, displayed in the `ui5-li-notification`.

**Note:** Use this for implementing actions.

**Note:** Should be used instead `u5-notification-action`, which is deprecated as of version 2.0.

nodes.label: importance
nodes.description: Defines the `Important` label of the item.

nodes.label: loading
nodes.description: Defines if a busy indicator would be displayed over the item.

nodes.label: loadingDelay
nodes.description: Defines the delay in milliseconds, after which the busy indicator will show up for this component.

nodes.label: read
nodes.description:
Defines if the `notification` is new or has been already read.

**Note:** if set to `false` the `titleText` has bold font,
if set to true - it has a normal font.

nodes.label: showClose
nodes.description: Defines if the `Close` button would be displayed.

nodes.label: state
nodes.description: Defines the status indicator of the item.

nodes.label: titleText
nodes.description: Defines the `titleText` of the item.

nodes.label: wrappingType
nodes.description:
Defines if the `titleText` and `description` should wrap,
they truncate by default.

**Note:** by default the `titleText` and `description`,
and a `ShowMore/Less` button would be displayed.

nodes.label: close
nodes.description: Fired when the `Close` button is pressed.

nodes.label: title-text
nodes.description: Used to style the titleText of the notification list item

nodes.label: ui5-page
nodes.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-page` is a container component that holds one whole screen of an application.
The page has three distinct areas that can hold content - a header, content area and a footer.
### Structure
#### Header
The top most area of the page is occupied by the header. The standard header includes a navigation button and a title.
#### Content
The content occupies the main part of the page. Only the content area is scrollable by default.
This can be prevented by setting `noScrolling` to `true`.
#### Footer
The footer is optional and occupies the part above the bottom part of the content. Alternatively, the footer can be fixed at the bottom of the page by enabling the `fixedFooter` property.

**Note:** `ui5-page` occipues the whole available space of its parent. In order to achieve the intended design you have to make sure
that there is enough space for the `ui5-page` to be rendered.
**Note:** In order for the `ui5-page` to be displayed, the parent element should have fixed height.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/Page.js";`

nodes.label: default
nodes.description: Defines the content HTML Element.

nodes.label: footer
nodes.description: Defines the footer HTML Element.

nodes.label: header
nodes.description: Defines the header HTML Element.

nodes.label: backgroundDesign
nodes.description:
Defines the background color of the `ui5-page`.

**Note:** When a ui5-list is placed inside the page, we recommend using “List” to ensure better color contrast.

nodes.label: fixedFooter
nodes.description:
Defines if the footer is fixed at the very bottom of the page.

**Note:** When set to true the footer is fixed at the very bottom of the page, otherwise it floats over the content with a slight offset from the bottom.

nodes.label: hideFooter
nodes.description: Defines the footer visibility.

nodes.label: noScrolling
nodes.description:
Disables vertical scrolling of page content.
If set to true, there will be no vertical scrolling at all.

nodes.label: content
nodes.description: Used to style the content section of the component

nodes.label: ui5-product-switch
nodes.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-product-switch` is an SAP Fiori specific web component that is used in `ui5-shellbar`
and allows the user to easily switch between products.

### Keyboard Handling
The `ui5-product-switch` provides advanced keyboard handling.
When focused, the user can use the following keyboard
shortcuts in order to perform a navigation:

- [Tab] - Move focus to the next interactive element after the `ui5-product-switch`
- [Up] or [Down] - Navigates up and down the items
- [Left] or [Right] - Navigates left and right the items

### ES6 Module Import
`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/ProductSwitch.js";`

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/ProductSwitchItem.js";` (for `ui5-product-switch-item`)

nodes.label: default
nodes.description: Defines the items of the `ui5-product-switch`.

nodes.label: ui5-product-switch-item
nodes.description:
### Overview
The `ui5-product-switch-item` web component represents the items displayed in the
`ui5-product-switch` web component.

**Note:** `ui5-product-switch-item` is not supported when used outside of `ui5-product-switch`.

### Keyboard Handling
The `ui5-product-switch` provides advanced keyboard handling.
When focused, the user can use the following keyboard
shortcuts in order to perform a navigation:

- [Space] / [Enter] or [Return] - Trigger `ui5-click` event

### ES6 Module Import
`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/ProductSwitchItem.js";`

nodes.label: image
nodes.description:
Defines an image to be displayed instead of the standard icon.

**Note:** The image slot takes precedence over the icon property.
**Note:** We recommend using non-interactive ui5-avatar with size S, Square shape and Transparent colorScheme for best alignment.

nodes.label: icon
nodes.description:
Defines the icon to be displayed as a graphical element within the component.

Example:

`<ui5-product-switch-item icon="palette">`

See all the available icons in the [Icon Explorer](https://sdk.openui5.org/test-resources/sap/m/demokit/iconExplorer/webapp/index.html).

nodes.label: subtitleText
nodes.description: Defines the subtitle of the component.

nodes.label: target
nodes.description:
Defines a target where the `targetSrc` content must be open.

Available options are:

- `_self`
- `_top`
- `_blank`
- `_parent`
- `_search`

**Note:** By default target will be open in the same frame as it was clicked.

nodes.label: targetSrc
nodes.description: Defines the component target URI. Supports standard hyperlink behavior.

nodes.label: titleText
nodes.description: Defines the title of the component.

nodes.label: click
nodes.description:
Fired when the `ui5-product-switch-item` is activated either with a
click/tap or by using the Enter or Space key.

nodes.label: ui5-search
nodes.description:
### Overview

A `ui5-search` is an input with suggestions, used for user search.

The `ui5-search` consists of several elements parts:
- Scope - displays a select in the beggining of the component, used for filtering results by their scope.
- Input field - for user input value
- Clear button - gives the possibility for deleting the entered value
- Search button - a primary button for performing search, when the user has entered a search term
- Suggestions - a list with available search suggestions

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/Search.js";`

nodes.label: action
nodes.description: Defines the popup footer action button.

nodes.label: default
nodes.description: Defines the Search suggestion items.

nodes.label: filterButton
nodes.description:
Defines the filter button slot, used to display an additional filtering button.
This slot is intended for passing a `ui5-button` with a filter icon to provide extended filtering options.

**Note:** Scope button and Filter button are mutually exclusive.

nodes.label: illustration
nodes.description: Defines the illustrated message to be shown in the popup.

nodes.label: messageArea
nodes.description: Defines the illustrated message to be shown in the popup.

nodes.label: scopes
nodes.description: Defines the component scope options.

nodes.label: accessibleDescription
nodes.description: Defines the accessible ARIA description of the field.

nodes.label: accessibleName
nodes.description: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the component.

nodes.label: fieldLoading
nodes.description: Indicates whether a loading indicator should be shown in the input field.

nodes.label: loading
nodes.description: Indicates whether a loading indicator should be shown in the popup.

nodes.label: noTypeahead
nodes.description: Defines whether the value will be autcompleted to match an item.

nodes.label: open
nodes.description: Indicates whether the items picker is open.

nodes.label: placeholder
nodes.description:
Defines a short hint intended to aid the user with data entry when the
component has no value.

nodes.label: scopeValue
nodes.description:
Defines the value of the component:

Applications are responsible for setting the correct scope value.

**Note:** If the given value does not match any existing scopes,
no scope will be selected and the SearchField scope component will be displayed as empty.

nodes.label: showClearIcon
nodes.description: Defines whether the clear icon of the search will be shown.

nodes.label: value
nodes.description:
Defines the value of the component.

**Note:** The property is updated upon typing.

nodes.label: close
nodes.description: Fired when the popup is closed.

nodes.label: input
nodes.description: Fired when typing in input or clear icon is pressed.

nodes.label: open
nodes.description: Fired when the popup is opened.

nodes.label: scope-change
nodes.description: Fired when the scope has changed.

nodes.label: search
nodes.description: Fired when the user has triggered search with Enter key or Search Button press.

nodes.label: ui5-search-field
nodes.description:
### Overview

A `ui5-search-field` is an input field, used for user search.

The `ui5-search-field` consists of several elements parts:
- Scope - displays a select in the beggining of the component, used for filtering results by their scope.
- Input field - for user input value
- Clear button - gives the possibility for deleting the entered value
- Search button - a primary button for performing search, when the user has entered a search term

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/SearchField.js";`

nodes.label: filterButton
nodes.description:
Defines the filter button slot, used to display an additional filtering button.
This slot is intended for passing a `ui5-button` with a filter icon to provide extended filtering options.

**Note:** Scope button and Filter button are mutually exclusive.

nodes.label: scopes
nodes.description: Defines the component scope options.

nodes.label: accessibleDescription
nodes.description: Defines the accessible ARIA description of the field.

nodes.label: accessibleName
nodes.description: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the component.

nodes.label: fieldLoading
nodes.description: Indicates whether a loading indicator should be shown in the input field.

nodes.label: placeholder
nodes.description:
Defines a short hint intended to aid the user with data entry when the
component has no value.

nodes.label: scopeValue
nodes.description:
Defines the value of the component:

Applications are responsible for setting the correct scope value.

**Note:** If the given value does not match any existing scopes,
no scope will be selected and the SearchField scope component will be displayed as empty.

nodes.label: showClearIcon
nodes.description: Defines whether the clear icon of the search will be shown.

nodes.label: value
nodes.description:
Defines the value of the component.

**Note:** The property is updated upon typing.

nodes.label: input
nodes.description: Fired when typing in input or clear icon is pressed.

nodes.label: scope-change
nodes.description: Fired when the scope has changed.

nodes.label: search
nodes.description: Fired when the user has triggered search with Enter key or Search Button press.

nodes.label: ui5-search-item
nodes.description:
### Overview

A `ui5-search-item` is a list item, used for displaying search suggestions

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/SearchItem.js";`

nodes.label: actions
nodes.description:
Defines the actionable elements.
This slot allows placing additional interactive elements (such as buttons, icons, or tags)
next to the delete button, providing flexible customization for various user actions.

**Note:** While the slot is flexible, for consistency with design guidelines,
it's recommended to use `ui5-button` with `Transparent` design or `ui5-icon` elements.

nodes.label: image
nodes.description:
**Note:** While the slot allows the option of setting a custom avatar, to comply with the
design guidelines, use the `ui5-avatar` with size - XS.

nodes.label: deletable
nodes.description: Defines whether the search item is deletable.

nodes.label: description
nodes.description: Defines the description that appears right under the item text, if available.

nodes.label: icon
nodes.description:
Defines the icon name of the search item.
**Note:** If provided, the image slot will be ignored.

nodes.label: scopeName
nodes.description: Defines the scope of the search item

nodes.label: selected
nodes.description: Defines whether the search item is selected.

nodes.label: text
nodes.description: Defines the heading text of the search item.

nodes.label: delete
nodes.description: Fired when delete button is pressed.

nodes.label: ui5-search-item-group
nodes.description:
The `ui5-search-item-group` is type of suggestion item,
that can be used to split the `ui5-search-item` suggestions into groups.

nodes.label: default
nodes.description: Defines the items of the ui5-li-group.

nodes.label: header
nodes.description:
Defines the header of the component.

**Note:** Using this slot, the default header text of group and the value of `headerText` property will be overwritten.

nodes.label: headerAccessibleName
nodes.description: Defines the accessible name of the header.

nodes.label: headerText
nodes.description: Defines the header text of the ui5-li-group.

nodes.label: wrappingType
nodes.description:
Defines if the text of the component should wrap when it's too long. When set to "Normal", the content (title, description) will be wrapped using the `ui5-expandable-text` component.
The text can wrap up to 100 characters on small screens (size S) and up to 300 characters on larger screens (size M and above). When text exceeds these limits, it truncates with an ellipsis followed by a text expansion trigger. Available options are: - `None` (default) - The text will truncate with an ellipsis. - `Normal` - The text will wrap (without truncation).

nodes.label: move
nodes.description:
Fired when a movable list item is dropped onto a drop target.

**Note:** `move` event is fired only if there was a preceding `move-over` with prevented default action.

nodes.label: move-over
nodes.description:
Fired when a movable list item is moved over a potential drop target during a dragging operation.

If the new position is valid, prevent the default action of the event using `preventDefault()`.

nodes.label: header
nodes.description: Used to style the header item of the group

nodes.label: title
nodes.description: Used to style the title of the group header

nodes.label: ui5-search-item-show-more
nodes.description:
### Overview

A `ui5-search-item-show-more` is a special type of ui5-li that acts as a button to progressively reveal additional (overflow) items within a group.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/SearchItemShowMore.js";`

nodes.label: itemsToShowCount
nodes.description:
Specifies the number of additional items available to show.
If no value is defined, the control shows "Show more" (without any counter).
If a number is provided, it displays "Show more (N)", where N is that number.

nodes.label: selected
nodes.description: Defines whether the show more item is selected.

nodes.label: click
nodes.description:
Fired when the component is activated, either with a mouse/tap
or by pressing the Enter or Space keys.

nodes.label: ui5-search-message-area
nodes.description: `import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/SearchMessageArea.js";`

nodes.label: description
nodes.description: Defines the description text to be displayed.

nodes.label: text
nodes.description: Defines the text to be displayed.

nodes.label: ui5-search-scope
nodes.description: The `ui5-search-scope` represents the options for the scope in `ui5-search`.

nodes.label: text
nodes.description: Defines the text of the component.

nodes.label: value
nodes.description:
Defines the value of the `ui5-search-scope`.
Used for selection in Search scopes.

nodes.label: ui5-shellbar
nodes.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-shellbar` is meant to serve as an application header
and includes numerous built-in features, such as: logo, profile image/icon, title, search field, notifications and so on.

### Stable DOM Refs

You can use the following stable DOM refs for the `ui5-shellbar`:

- logo
- notifications
- overflow
- profile
- product-switch

### Keyboard Handling

#### Fast Navigation
This component provides a build in fast navigation group which can be used via [F6] / [Shift] + [F6] / [Ctrl] + [Alt/Option] / [Down] or [Ctrl] + [Alt/Option] + [Up].
In order to use this functionality, you need to import the following module:
`import "@ui5/webcomponents-base/dist/features/F6Navigation.js"`

### ES6 Module Import
`import "@ui5/webcomponents-fiori/dist/ShellBar.js";`

nodes.label: assistant
nodes.description: Defines the assistant slot.

nodes.label: branding
nodes.description:
Defines the branding slot.
The `ui5-shellbar-branding` component is intended to be placed inside this slot.
Content placed here takes precedence over the `primaryTitle` property and the `logo` content slot.

**Note:** The `branding` slot is in an experimental state and is a subject to change.

nodes.label: content
nodes.description:
Define the items displayed in the content area.

Use the `data-hide-order` attribute with numeric value to specify the order of the items to be hidden when the space is not enough.
Lower values will be hidden first.

**Note:** The `content` slot is in an experimental state and is a subject to change.

nodes.label: default
nodes.description:
Defines the `ui5-shellbar` additional items.

**Note:**
You can use the `<ui5-shellbar-item></ui5-shellbar-item>`.

nodes.label: logo
nodes.description:
Defines the logo of the `ui5-shellbar`.
For example, you can use `ui5-avatar` or `img` elements as logo.

nodes.label: menuItems
nodes.description:
Defines the items displayed in menu after a click on a start button.

**Note:** You can use the  `<ui5-li></ui5-li>` and its ancestors.

nodes.label: profile
nodes.description:
You can pass `ui5-avatar` to set the profile image/icon.
If no profile slot is set - profile will be excluded from actions.

**Note:** We recommend not using the `size` attribute of `ui5-avatar` because
it should have specific size by design in the context of `ui5-shellbar` profile.

nodes.label: searchField
nodes.description: Defines the `ui5-input`, that will be used as a search field.

nodes.label: startButton
nodes.description:
Defines a `ui5-button` in the bar that will be placed in the beginning.
We encourage this slot to be used for a menu button.
It gets overstyled to match ShellBar's styling.

nodes.label: accessibilityAttributes
nodes.description:
Defines additional accessibility attributes on different areas of the component.

The accessibilityAttributes object has the following fields,
where each field is an object supporting one or more accessibility attributes:

- **logo** - `logo.role` and `logo.name`.
- **notifications** - `notifications.expanded` and `notifications.hasPopup`.
- **profile** - `profile.expanded`, `profile.hasPopup` and `profile.name`.
- **product** - `product.expanded` and `product.hasPopup`.
- **search** - `search.hasPopup`.
- **overflow** - `overflow.expanded` and `overflow.hasPopup`.
- **branding** - `branding.name`.

The accessibility attributes support the following values:

- **role**: Defines the accessible ARIA role of the logo area.
Accepts the following string values: `button` or `link`.

- **expanded**: Indicates whether the button, or another grouping element it controls,
is currently expanded or collapsed.
Accepts the following string values: `true` or `false`.

- **hasPopup**: Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element,
such as menu or dialog, that can be triggered by the button.

Accepts the following string values: `dialog`, `grid`, `listbox`, `menu` or `tree`.
- **name**: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the area.
Accepts any string.

nodes.label: disableSearchCollapse
nodes.description:
Disables the automatic search field expansion/collapse when the available space is not enough.

**Note:** The `disableSearchCollapse` property is in an experimental state and is a subject to change.

nodes.label: hideSearchButton
nodes.description:
Defines the visibility state of the search button.

**Note:** The `hideSearchButton` property is in an experimental state and is a subject to change.

nodes.label: logoDomRef
nodes.description: Returns the `logo` DOM ref.

nodes.label: notificationsCount
nodes.description:
Defines the `notificationsCount`,
displayed in the notification icon top-right corner.

nodes.label: notificationsDomRef
nodes.description: Returns the `notifications` icon DOM ref.

nodes.label: overflowDomRef
nodes.description: Returns the `overflow` icon DOM ref.

nodes.label: primaryTitle
nodes.description:
Defines the `primaryTitle`.

**Note:** The `primaryTitle` would be hidden on S screen size (less than approx. 700px).

nodes.label: productSwitchDomRef
nodes.description: Returns the `product-switch` icon DOM ref.

nodes.label: profileDomRef
nodes.description: Returns the `profile` icon DOM ref.

nodes.label: secondaryTitle
nodes.description:
Defines the `secondaryTitle`.

**Note:** The `secondaryTitle` would be hidden on S and M screen sizes (less than approx. 1300px).

nodes.label: showNotifications
nodes.description: Defines, if the notification icon would be displayed.

nodes.label: showProductSwitch
nodes.description: Defines, if the product switch icon would be displayed.

nodes.label: showSearchField
nodes.description:
Defines, if the Search Field would be displayed when there is a valid `searchField` slot.

**Note:** By default the Search Field is not displayed.

nodes.label: content-item-visibility-change
nodes.description:
Fired, when an item from the content slot is hidden or shown.
**Note:** The `content-item-visibility-change` event is in an experimental state and is a subject to change.

nodes.label: logo-click
nodes.description: Fired, when the logo is activated.

nodes.label: menu-item-click
nodes.description:
Fired, when a menu item is activated

**Note:** You can prevent closing of overflow popover by calling `event.preventDefault()`.

nodes.label: notifications-click
nodes.description: Fired, when the notification icon is activated.

nodes.label: product-switch-click
nodes.description:
Fired, when the product switch icon is activated.

**Note:** You can prevent closing of overflow popover by calling `event.preventDefault()`.

nodes.label: profile-click
nodes.description: Fired, when the profile slot is present.

nodes.label: search-button-click
nodes.description:
Fired, when the search button is activated.

**Note:** You can prevent expanding/collapsing of the search field by calling `event.preventDefault()`.

nodes.label: search-field-clear
nodes.description:
Fired, when the search cancel button is activated.

**Note:** You can prevent the default behavior (clearing the search field value) by calling `event.preventDefault()`. The search will still be closed.
**Note:** The `search-field-clear` event is in an experimental state and is a subject to change.

nodes.label: search-field-toggle
nodes.description: Fired, when the search field is expanded or collapsed.

nodes.label: root
nodes.description: Used to style the outermost wrapper of the `ui5-shellbar`

nodes.label: ui5-shellbar-branding
nodes.description:
### Overview
The `ui5-shellbar-branding` component is intended to be placed inside the branding slot of the
`ui5-shellbar` component. Its content has higher priority than the `primaryTitle` property
and the `logo` slot of `ui5-shellbar`.

**Note:** Although this slot accepts HTML Elements, it is strongly recommended that you only use text in order to preserve the intended design.

If not provided a default text alternative will be set, if present.

**Notes:**

- `_self`

**This property must only be used when the `href` property is set.**

The accessibility attributes support the following values:

- **expanded**: Indicates whether the button, or another grouping element it controls,
is currently expanded or collapsed.
Accepts the following string values: `true` or `false`.

- **hasPopup**: Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element,
such as menu or dialog, that can be triggered by the button.

- **controls**: Identifies the element (or elements) whose contents
or presence are controlled by the component.
Accepts a lowercase string value, referencing the ID of the element it controls.

Defines the filter button slot, used to display an additional filtering button.
This slot is intended for passing a `ui5-button` with a filter icon to provide extended filtering options.

nodes.label: accessibleDescription

nodes.label: accessibleName

nodes.label: fieldLoading
nodes.description: Indicates whether a loading indicator should be shown in the input field.

nodes.label: loading
nodes.description: Indicates whether a loading indicator should be shown in the popup.

nodes.label: noTypeahead
nodes.description: Defines whether the value will be autcompleted to match an item.

nodes.label: open
nodes.description: Indicates whether the items picker is open.

nodes.label: placeholder

Defines a short hint intended to aid the user with data entry when the
component has no value.

nodes.label: scopeValue

Defines the value of the component:

Applications are responsible for setting the correct scope value.

**Note:** If the given value does not match any existing scopes,
no scope will be selected and the SearchField scope component will be displayed as empty.

nodes.label: showClearIcon
nodes.description: Defines whether the clear icon of the search will be shown.

nodes.label: value
nodes.description:
Defines the value of the component.

**Note:** The property is updated upon typing.

nodes.label: close
nodes.description: Fired when the popup is closed.

nodes.label: input
nodes.description: Fired when typing in input or clear icon is pressed.

nodes.label: scope-change
